title: Subfactor planar algebra
summary: A planar algebra is the standard combinatorial structure encoding the higher-relative-commutant tower of a finite-index subfactor. It provides diagrammatic composition rules that replace algebraic multiplication in the standard invariant.
assumptions: The subfactor has finite Jones index.
Standard invariant is the object of interest, not the individual factors.

boundary_conditions: Jones index is finite and explicitly computable

applies_to: classification of finite-index subfactors via standard invariant
out_of_scope: full von Neumann algebra structure beyond the standard invariant

citations: V. F. R. Jones, Planar algebras I (1999)

promotion.rationale: Seed unit for formal-derivation lane: subfactor planar algebra as reusable concept for Jones/von Neumann algebra topics.

payload.definition: A planar algebra is a graded vector space P_n (n >= 0) with composition maps defined by planar tangles. For a subfactor N subset M of finite index, the n-box spaces P_n = N' cap M_{n-1} form a planar algebra whose structure encodes the standard invariant.
payload.operational_role: Diagrammatic calculus for computing the standard invariant without explicit algebraic manipulation of individual factors.
